qa_pairs.question: How can I create model checkpoints when training with Keras in TensorFlow?
qa_pairs.answer: To create checkpoints with Keras, use the `tf.keras.callbacks.ModelCheckpoint` callback with the `model.fit()` method. For example, to save a snapshot at the end of every training epoch, you would define `callbacks = [tf.keras.callbacks.ModelCheckpoint(filepath="./ckpt", save_freq="epoch")]` and then pass these callbacks to your `model.fit()` call. Remember to ensure that the specified `filepath` exists.

qa_pairs.question: What is the solution for the `OMP: Error #15` when `libiomp5.so` is already initialized, especially when using scikit-image with TensorFlow?
Are there any known performance issues with `tf.keras.layers.Random` data augmentation layers in TensorFlow, and if so, how can they be avoided?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, there was a bug in the Keras implementation of TensorFlow after version 2.8.3 that significantly slowed down the training process (by more than 100 times) when using data augmentation layers prefixed with `tf.keras.layers.Random` (e.g., `tf.keras.layers.RandomRotation`, `tf.keras.layers.RandomTranslation`). This bug has since been fixed in version 2.12. To avoid this issue, ensure you are using TensorFlow version 2.12 or newer, or stick to versions 2.8.3 or older if you rely heavily on these layers.
